title: The Silken Snake.
content:
For sport my Julia threw a lace
    Of silk and silver at my face:
    Watchet the silk was, and did make
    A show as if't had been a snake:
    The suddenness did me afright,
    But though it scar'd, it did not bite.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "The Silken Snake.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
